key_insight: EVMDOT turns dynamic optimal transport into an unconstrained energetic variational problem, enabling direct reconstruction of energy and Waddington landscapes from time-series density data.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a geometry-aware, data-driven way to infer hidden dynamical structure in complex systems, useful for physics-informed learning and biomedical state-transition analysis.
method: Use a flow map to automatically satisfy the continuity equation without explicit constraints.
Combine least action and maximum dissipation principles to recover the velocity field from data.
Apply the framework to Fokker--Planck dynamics to estimate energy landscapes from density trajectories.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments and an ADNI application showing robust landscape recovery and a two-well disease progression structure from time-series density data.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quantity-quality trade-off in the observations, so sparse or noisy time windows may still challenge accurate landscape reconstruction.
paper_title: Learning the Energy Landscapes of Dynamical Systems via Energetic Variational Optimal Transport under Data Quantity--Quality Trade-offs
